name: HubSpot API
what_it_does: HubSpot's CRM and marketing automation platform API for managing contacts, companies, deals, tickets, and marketing workflows with an official MCP server.
use_cases: Creating and updating CRM contacts and deals from agent workflows
Sales pipeline management and deal tracking
Customer support ticket creation and routing
not_for: Teams on tight budgets (HubSpot paid plans are expensive)
Simple contact management without marketing automation needs
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a HubSpot CRM for sales/marketing automation with natural language through the official MCP server.
avoid_when: You need a lightweight CRM or don't already use HubSpot.

auth.notes: Private apps use bearer token (API key). Public apps use OAuth2 with fine-grained scopes. MCP server uses private app token. OAuth2 needed for multi-tenant scenarios.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free CRM with basic contact management, 1M contacts
pricing.notes: Free CRM is genuinely full-featured for basic use. API access is available on free tier with rate limits. Advanced automation requires paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: 100-150 requests/10 seconds depending on plan, burst to 200

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Good error messages with category and message fields. Developer documentation is comprehensive and well-maintained.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Batch operations support idempotent upserts. Individual creates are not idempotent by default but unique property matching helps.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits of 100-150/10s can be exhausted quickly by agents processing large contact lists
API versioning: v1, v2, v3 APIs coexist with different patterns - use v3/crm for modern work
Association APIs are complex - creating contacts, companies, and deals requires separate association calls
MCP server is community-maintained, not officially from HubSpot engineering team
Property validation is strict - custom properties must be created before use
